```{r}
library(ggdendro)
library(dendextend)

# Compute correlation matrix and convert to distance matrix
cor_matrix <- cor(data[, env.var], use = "complete.obs")
dist_matrix <- as.dist(1 - cor_matrix)  # Use 1 - correlation directly for distance

# Perform hierarchical clustering
hclust_result <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "ward.D2")

# Convert the result to a dendrogram object
dend <- as.dendrogram(hclust_result)

# Replace labels with custom labels
labels(dend) <- custom_labels[labels(dend)]

# Highlight branches based on correlation threshold
cor_threshold <- 0.6
cor_pairs <- which(abs(cor_matrix) > cor_threshold, arr.ind = TRUE)

highlight_branches <- function(dend, cor_pairs) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(cor_pairs)) {
    idx1 <- cor_pairs[i, 1]
    idx2 <- cor_pairs[i, 2]
    dend <- dendextend::set(dend, "branches_col", value = "red", k = 7)
  }
  return(dend)
}

# Apply highlighting
dend <- highlight_branches(dend, cor_pairs)

# Customize and plot the dendrogram
dend <- dend %>%
  color_branches(k = 7) %>%  # Color branches for 7 clusters
  set("labels_cex", 0.8)     # Adjust label size

par(mar = c(8, 3, 4, 4))  # Adjust margins for better label visibility

# Adjust y-axis to display correlation
plot(dend, 
     main = "    Hierarchical Clustering of Variables", 
     ylab = "1 - Correlation", 
     ylim = c(0, 0.5))
abline(h = 0.4, col = "gray", lty = 2, lwd = 2)

# Add a secondary y-axis with correlation values
axis(side = 4, at = seq(0, 1, by = 0.2), 
     labels = round(1 - seq(0, 1, by = 0.2), 2))
mtext("Correlation", side = 4, line = 3)

```
